results.question: I have never heard of Intelligent Robotics. What is this degree about?
results.expected_answer: The Bachelor of Science (Honours) Intelligent Robotics is a 3-year programme that strikes an exquisite balance between the fundamentals of engineering and hands-on practical skills. This multidisciplinary programme combines electronics, robotics, artificial intelligence, automation, and computer programming. It adopts a modern learning approach with early exposure to real-world applications. Graduates will be agile knowledge workers in the IR4.0 age and beyond, highly sought after by the industry.

It adopts a modern learning approach with early exposure to real-world applications. Graduates will be agile knowledge workers in the IR4.0 age and beyond, highly sought after by the industry.

results.question: Show the full subject outline for ARM6113 Technical Calculus.?
results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARM6113 Title: Technical Calculus Category: core Group: technical Year: 1 Trimester: 1 Intake Cycle Label: March/April 2026 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 48 hours (Lecture Hours = 35 and Supervised Tutorial Hours = 13) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To provide necessary grounding in calculus, algebra, vectors, complex functions, Fourier analysis and probability to students pursuing engineering and science degrees. Assessment: - Tests/Quizzes: 40% - Final Examination: 60% Course Contents: - Calculus of one variable - Fourier series - Complex number functions and vector algebra - Probability Course Learning Outcomes: - Compute the limits, derivatives, integrals and extrema of single-variable functions. - Perform basic vector operations and calculate the powers and roots of complex numbers. - Construct the power series and the Fourier series representations of functions. - Determine the probability, mean and variance for various distributions in discrete and continuous random variables. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx
results.ui_answer: Subject Code: Title: Technical Calculus Category: core Group: technical Year: 1 Trimester: 1 Intake Cycle Label: March/April 2026 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 48 hours (Lecture Hours = 35 and Supervised Tutorial Hours = 13) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To provide necessary grounding in calculus, algebra, vectors, complex functions, Fourier analysis and probability to students pursuing engineering and science degrees. Assessment: - Tests/Quizzes: 40% - Final Examination: 60% Course Contents: - Calculus of one variable - Fourier series - Complex number functions and vector algebra - Probability Course Learning Outcomes: - Compute the limits, derivatives, integrals and extrema of single-variable functions. - Perform basic vector operations and calculate the powers and roots of complex numbers. - Construct the power series and the Fourier series representations of functions. - Determine the probability, mean and variance for various distributions in discrete and continuous random variables. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx
